title: frangula cambodiana
intro: Frangula cambodiana, also known as the Cambodian buckthorn, is a shrub or small tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its thorny branches and small, yellowish-green flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its laxative properties, derived from its sennoside-rich fruit. The medicinal properties of Frangula cambodiana are attributed to its ability to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation, making it a valuable remedy for digestive issues. Frangula cambodiana can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or capsules, and has also been used in traditional remedies to treat fever, inflammation, and skin conditions.
